MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-19-2017 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE AND RESET TRIAL DEADLINES. CAMILO ARTIGA-PURCELL, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ARAY RAY JABAGCHOURIAN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS PATRICK REILLY AND KISMATIC, INC. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 9/25/17 IS CONTINUED TO 2/20/18 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY WILL REMAIN CLOSED UNLESS OPENED BY DULY NOTICED MOTION. TO THE EXTENT DISCOVERY IS OPENED, IT SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE FOLLOWING AREAS: 1)COMPLETION OF THE DEPOSITION OF PATRICK REILLY, 2)DISCOVERY RE KISMATIC BANK STATEMENTS, 3)DEPOSITION OF MR. JACKS, FORMER EMPLOYEE OF KISMATIC, 4)DISCOVERY RE APPRENDA AND 5)EXECUTED DOCUMENTS FROM KISMATIC RE FORMATION, PLAINTIFF'S EQUITY, PURPORTAL TERMINATION, AND APPRENDA ACQUISITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Joint Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Protective Order
Off Calendar Oct-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

DEFENDANTS KISMATIC INC. and PATRICK REILLY's Joint Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Protective Order is OFF CALENDAR per agreement of the parties.

Judge Pro Tem: Dianne Peebles; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Continued To Feb-20-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARA JABAGCHOURIAN, TEL. NO. 1-650-437-6840 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Kismatic, Inc.'s motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. While not the strongest case for relief per the discretionary provisions of CCP 473(b), Kismatic has shown that it failed to respond to the complaint due to inadvertence and/or excusable neglect. Granting the motion is in accord with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff Burness Broussard had not identified any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/HK

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-25-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
